definitions.text: An indefinite, but usually small, number of.
examples: There are a few cars (=some, but a relatively small number) in the street.
Quite a few people (=a significant number) were pleasantly surprised.

definitions.text: Not many; a small (in comparison with another number stated or implied) but somewhat indefinite number of.
examples: Very few did she have not to go there, did she?
I was expecting a big crowd at the party, but very few people (=almost none) turned up.

definitions.text: Obscuring one to two oktas (eighths) of the sky.
examples: Tonight: A few clouds. Increasing cloudiness overnight.

NOAA definition of the term few clouds: An official sky cover classification for aviation weather observations, descriptive of a sky cover of 1/8 to 2/8. This is applied only when obscuring phenomena aloft are present--that is, not when obscuring phenomena are surface-based, such as fog.
Many are called, but few are chosen.
